video:video.video:title: Building Blocks - Junctions - TheDuchy ®
video:video.video:description:
One very common activity when rigging is when deciding what to do when two ropes cross each other. Which direction do you want them to go as they move away from the junction? This is something that you may not a thought about if you’ve always been tying to some standard pattern, but when you start to try to create on your own, understanding a few basic building blocks can be extremely helpful. Here are a few options when two ropes cross each other and how they are all related…

video:video.video:title: Redmayne Catch - TheDuchy ®
video:video.video:description:
The Prusik knot is one of the coolest knots in bondage. It is the key to a number of rapid-capture ties. Dispite that, it is not well known.
This Video Teaches you 2 ways to make this knot and 4 cool things you can do with it! I included timestamps if you want to jump right to a particular topic :)
• Two methods to make the Prusik Head (0:27 & 3:10) • Prusik Cuffs, a.k.a "Texas Handcuffs" (4:30) -- If applied correctly, these are inescapable! • Prusik Shackle (8:57) -- An single-column tie that can be applied in *5 seconds* • Prusik Anchor (11:16) -- Allow the top to rapidly adjust how tightly the bottom is lashed to the bed or into a frame • Redmayne Catch (14:23) -- Allows the bottom to tighten their own restraints, but not to loosen them (WARNING: This is a dangerous edge play device that may only be used under supervision.)
-------------------------- • For a step-by-step pictorial tutorial in addition to this video, check out: www.TheDuchy.com/redmayne-catch/ • Become a Member and help me create more videos like this! www.TheDuchy.com/join/ • I also have videos for Members only! They can only be seen on TheDuchy.com!

video:video.video:title: Flogger Forge - Cored Square Knot (CSK) - TheDuchy ®
video:video.video:description:
Flogger Forge! This knot can be used as a component when building your own beautiful floggers out of rope!
A Cored Square Knot can be a great way to create a rope strap. Perhaps you can use it at the end of your falls or as a way to create a loop at the end of the handle of your flogger. This can also be a decorative and very secure way to use up a short amount of rope at the end of a bondage tie.
For more such knots, and for complete plans and demonstrations for tying your own flogger, check out: https://www.TheDuchy.com/flogger-forge/

Plan: 9s – 2MRK6 < CS6+3 < MWK9 > 9F(BK4)
For more such knots, and for complete plans and demonstrations for tying your own flogger, check out: https://www.TheDuchy.com/flogger-forge/

The Gravity Boot- Style Single Column is a fast and stylish way to tie your partners ankles to an anchor (…perhaps bed posts…). With this tie, the tension can be added in line with the leg, out the sole of your partner's foot, and that tension will be felt evenly around the foot and ankle instead of digging in to one side as is the case with most other single column ties. This version is faster than the real Gravity Boot, but it is not suitable for suspension.

video:video.video:title: Shinju - TheDuchy ®
video:video.video:description:
This classic chest harness, known and practiced by riggers throughout the world, is simple, secure and, when applied properly, can be used for suspension. Using this as your primary upper-body suspension attachment harness means that your partner's weight is supported by their rib cage and not their arms, reducing the risk of radial nerve injury so common with the standard TK (Takate Kote). Also check out the Suspension Shinju, an update that is even more interconnected and good for suspension.

video:video.video:title: Flogger Forge - 2-Strand Diamond Knot (DK2) - TheDuchy ®

video:video.video:title: Flogger Forge - 1-Strand Diamond Knot (DK1) - TheDuchy ®
video:video.video:description:
Flogger Forge! This knot can be used as a component when building your own beautiful floggers out of rope!
The Single Strand Diamond Knot, sometimes also called a Celtic Button Knot, can be used to add a knot at the end of your Falls that is heavier and more decorative than a Blood Knot. It could also be used on cords that come out of the bottom of your handle to add some flair.
For more such knots, and for complete plans and demonstrations for tying your own flogger, check out: https://www.TheDuchy.com/flogger-forge/

video:video.video:title: Flogger Forge - Cored Granny Knot (CGK) - TheDuchy ®
video:video.video:description:
Flogger Forge! This knot can be used as a component when building your own beautiful floggers out of rope! Go learn the Cored Square Knot first. The Cored Granny Knot is tied almost exactly the same way as is the Cored Square Knot, with only one tiny variation. But the results make it look dramatically different. This creates a twisted strap instead of a flat strap. This can be a great way to add vicious texture to the end of your falls.
For more such knots, and for complete plans and demonstrations for tying your own flogger, check out: https://www.TheDuchy.com/flogger-forge/

video:video.video:description:
Flogger Forge! This knot can be used as a component when building your own beautiful floggers out of rope! The Blood Knot--a more dramatic name for a simple Double Overhand Knot (or triple, quadruple, etc.)--is a simple way to add weight and texture to the falls of your flogger, making it more ... impactful...
For more such knots, and for complete plans and demonstrations for tying your own flogger, check out: https://www.theduchy.com/flogger-forge/

video:video.video:title: Flogger Forge - Braid - TheDuchy ®
video:video.video:description:

It can also act as additional texture for the falls of your flogger (If you bracket it some stable transition knot like the 3-Strand Matthew Walker Knot for example).
For more such knots, and for complete plans and demonstrations for tying your own flogger, check out: https://www.theduchy.com/flogger-forge/

video:video.video:title: L Friction - TheDuchy ®
video:video.video:description:
The L Friction reaches slightly sideways to anchor one line to another. It is useful where the line being anchored would tend to move one direction (say more toward the hands) and you would like to prevent it from doing so AND the line doing the friction is not directly parallel to the line being anchored.
